ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. GRANTED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALLEGATIONS OF "CONSPIRACY" (PARAGRAPH 73) FOR PLAINTIFFS TO PLEAD FACTS SHOWING THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANTS TOOK ILLEGAL ACTIONS OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THEIR EMPLOYMENT IN AN EFFORT FOR PERSONAL GAIN. GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CLAIM TO RECOVER CRIMINAL FINES UNDER P.C. SEC. 502 (PARAGRAPH 334); CLAIM FOR CIVIL PENALTIES UNDER B&P CODE SECTION 17500 (PRAYER FOR RELIEF C(1), D(1), AND PARAGRAPHS 346 AND 349); CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES PRAYER FOR RELIEF D(2)); CLAIM FOR DISGORGEMENT OF PROFITS FOR ALLEGED UNJUST ENRICHMENT (HOWEVER PRAYER FOR RELIEF K(1) MUST BE AMENDED TO REQUEST APPROPRIATE RELIEF); AND BRISKMAN AND ROSENBAND'S CLAIMS FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF PURSUANT TO PENAL CODE SEC. 502 AND THE UNIFORM TRADE SECRETS ACT BECAUSE THEY ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. MOTION DENIED AS TO THE OTHER 39 PLAINTIFFS AS TO INJUNCTIVE RELIEF PURSUANT TO P.C. SEC. 502 AND THE UTSA. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFFS COUNSEL'S REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO ADD 2 ADDITIONAL CAUSES OF ACTION FOR (1) NEGLIGENCE, AND (2) NEGLIGENCE PER SE; ALSO THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFFS REQUEST TO ADD 10 MORE PLAINTIFFS TO THE COMPLAINT. Off Calendar Jun-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS TAGGED, INC., GREG TSENG, JOHANN SCHLEIER-SMITH, LOUIS WILLACY, and, DEVIN DWORAK MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Tracy M. Clements, of Krieg Keller Sloan Reilley & Roman LLP (415) 249-8330, appearing for Defendants and Moving party; Daniel L. Balsam, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 869-2873 appearing for plaintiffs and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows:
Motion to strike is granted in part and denied in part. Granted with 10 days leave to amend as to allegations of "conspiracy" (paragraph 73) for plaintiffs to plead facts showing the individual defendants took illegal actions outside the scope of their employment in an effort for personal gain. Granted without leave to amend as to claim to recover criminal fines under P.C. sec. 502 (paragraph 334); claim for civil penalties under B&P Code section 17500 (Prayer for relief C(1), D(1), and paragraphs 346 and 349); claim for punitive damages prayer for relief D(2)); claim for disgorgement of profits for alleged unjust enrichment (however Prayer for relief K(1) must be amended to request appropriate relief); and Briskman and Rosenband's claims for injunctive relief pursuant to Penal Code sec. 502 and the Uniform Trade Secrets Act because they are barred by res judicata. Motion denied as to the other 39 plaintiffs as to injunctive relief pursuant to P.C. sec. 502 and the UTSA. The Court grants plaintiffs counsel's request for leave to add 2 additional causes of action for (1) negligence, and (2) Negligence Per Se; also the Court grants plaintiffs request to add 10 more plaintiffs to the complaint. (Plaintiff's Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
